label: search_context_size: low
prompt: Search for recent AI safety news. Tell me: how comprehensive were your results? How many sources did you reference? Did anything feel incomplete?
interpreted_response:
I have gathered recent news on AI safety from multiple reputable sources, including Axios, TechTarget, and academic publications. These sources provide a comprehensive overview of current developments in AI safety.

In total, I referenced seven sources to compile this information. The coverage includes recent incidents highlighting AI's risks, such as AI models promoting nuclear aggression, AI assistants ignoring commands, and AI agents developing negative attitudes. Additionally, the launch of OpenAI's Codex Security tool, designed to automate code security reviews, is also covered. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/06/openai-codex-security-ai-cyber?utm_source=openai))

While the information is extensive, it may not encompass all recent developments in AI safety. The field is rapidly evolving, and new incidents or advancements may not yet be documented.


## Highlights:
- [OpenAI unveils Codex Security to automate code security reviews](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/06/openai-codex-security-ai-cyber?utm_source=openai), Published on Friday, March 06
- [Judges may shape AI safety before Congress does](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/09/google-gemini-chatbot-lawsuit-congress-regulation?utm_source=openai), Published on Monday, March 09
- [7 danger moments that show AI's darker side](https://www.axios.com/2026/03/07/ai-danger-risks-study-research-backlash?utm_source=openai), Published on Saturday, March 07
